title: user — model relationships
description: User is the aggregate root. Profile is a 1:1 satellite (owns_one). TenantMember is a many-to-many pivot between User and Tenant. Every FK is enforced at the DB layer; onDelete policy per row balances audit-preserving RESTRICT (to identities + applications + profiles) with tenant-scoped CASCADE (to tenants + roles + users when the parent goes away).

internal.description: 1:1 PII satellite. RESTRICT so a Profile is never orphaned by a hasty User delete — the profile cleanup path is CleanupOrphanedProfilesJob after a hard-delete grace period.

Inverse. `Profile::user()` returns the User row that references it via `users.profile_id`. Loose coupling — a Profile can exist briefly without a User during provisioning (created first, User then references it).
Every membership the User holds. Hard-delete of a User cascades their TenantMember rows so pivot rows never survive their principal.
`TenantMember::invitedBy()` — the User who created this membership (null for system-provisioned rows, e.g. tenant owner seeded on TenantProvisioned).

outbound.description: The global credential this User projects. RESTRICT so an Identity delete requires every projection to be soft-deleted first (GDPR erasure workflow — see `PurgeErasedUsersJob`).
The Application this User is scoped to. RESTRICT so decommissioning an Application requires all Users to be re-provisioned or erased first.

Home tenant. CASCADE aligns with tenancy's TenantErased contract: erasing a Tenant erases the tenant-scoped Users belonging to it. Multi-tenant Users survive via their remaining TenantMember rows — the `home` tenant_id migrates to another membership on erasure (see TenantMemberObserver.tenantErased).
First-seen role for new-user routing. SET NULL on role delete so a role rename / retire doesn't cascade-delete User rows.
The Tenant this membership belongs to. Cascade with the tenant.
The role the User carries in this Tenant. RESTRICT so a role delete cannot silently downgrade every membership — the platform admin has to reassign first.

inbound.description: Domain models with a per-user ownership key compose the `BelongsToUser` trait shipped here. They store the linkage as `user_id` FK (nullable for domain rows that predate their principal, e.g. athletes without logins).
examples: sports::Athlete.user_id — nullable, populated only when the athlete has a login (teens + adults).
inbound.onDelete: RESTRICT by convention on domain FKs referencing users.id so a User erasure requires domain-side cleanup first (the `user:erase` command orchestrates the cascade explicitly per module).

accessor_chain.description: Callers rarely touch identity or profile directly — the User model surfaces the joined shape via accessors so the API contract stays flat.
